inputs.text: Main thakaan mehsoos kar rahi hoon, bukhar bhi hai, sir dard bhi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Although possible, not enough specific symptoms to suggest Typhoid at this stage

inputs.text: Bukhar roz badh raha hai, pet dard bhi ho raha hai, kabz bhi hai

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Abdominal pain and constipation are common in Gastroenteritis

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Fever is still present, but other symptoms are becoming more prominent

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Step-wise fever and abdominal pain could be indicative of Typhoid, but more specific symptoms are needed for a higher ranking

inputs.text: Tez bukhar hai, kabz ke baad dast ho gaya hai, bahut kamzori mehsoos ho rahi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Tez bukhar, kabz ke baad dast, and severe weakness are cardinal signs of Typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Although abdominal symptoms are present, the pattern of fever and weakness is more suggestive of Typhoid

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Malaria typically presents with a more cyclical pattern of fever and chills, which is not seen here

inputs.text: Haal hi mein haalat aur bigad gaya hai, bukhar ab bhi hai, thakaan bhi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Worsening condition with persistent high fever, and risk of intestinal perforation confirms the diagnosis of Typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the progression of symptoms and the presence of blood-tinged sputum

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the lack of cyclical fever pattern and specific symptoms
